If you do it, you must not do it. This makes a clear way to find the right choice. It stops people from picking a thing they do that would be good no matter what the end is. So my answer is: This way works because it shows a thing you do that is always good, no matter what the end is. It stops people from picking a thing you do that hurts them or is not right, because they cannot make a choice that is always good when every choice is the best one.

english_query: when we have good reason to think a thing will or will not happen
ugf_response: , we first look at what we know about the world. We see a lot of things that have happened before. For a case, if we see dark clouds and feel wind, we have good reason to think rain will come. This is because the past shows us a way that things happen again and again. On the other hand, if we see a friend walk into a room and see a car stop, we have good reason to think the friend will not go there. The way things happen again and again in the past shows us that the same way things happen again and again gives us a good reason to think the thing will or will not happen again. This is not a guess; it is a guess made from what we have seen. Some might say that even with good reason, the thing could stop for another reason, so the guess is not sure. But the more good reason we have, the more sure we feel. So the main way is that we have good reason, and the guess we have is strong enough to trust that the thing will or will not happen. So my answer is: When we have good reason to think a thing will or will not happen, we can be sure it will or will not happen.
